prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. On December 29, 2013, three journalists working for the Qatari-based international news channel Al Jazeera English, Australian Peter Greste, Canadian Mohamed Fahmy and Egyptian Baher Mohamed were taken into custody by Egyptian security forces at the Marriott Hotel in Cairo following a raid at their room, which was used for the news channel's remote studio. The Egyptian Interior Ministry confirmed the arrest and said the journalists were accused of reporting false news which was "damaging to national security".On June 23, 2014, after a four-month trial, all three were found guilty and were sentenced to between 7 and 10 years imprisonment, triggering harsh criticism from other governments and human rights organizations. A day later, Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi declared that he would not interfere with judicial rulings.In February
B. After Egyptian security forces raided the Marriott Hotel in Cairo on December 29, 2013, three journalists from Al Jazeera English, Australian Peter Greste, Canadian Mohamed Fahmy, and Egyptian Baher Mohamed were arrested and charged with reporting false news that endangered national security. The Egyptian Interior Ministry confirmed the arrest and sentenced all three to seven to ten years in prison after a four-month trial. Other governments and human rights groups expressed outrage and Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi declared that he would not interfere on judicial decisions.
C. On the 29th of December, 2013, three journalists, all employed by Al Jazeera English, an international news channel based in Qatar, were apprehended by Egyptian security personnel. These individuals were Peter Greste, an Australian citizen, Mohamed Fahmy, a Canadian national, and Baher Mohamed, an Egyptian. Their apprehension occurred at the Marriott Hotel in Cairo, subsequent to a search of their room, which had been serving as a makeshift studio for the news channel. The Egyptian Interior Ministry subsequently verified the arrests, stating that the journalists faced allegations of disseminating inaccurate information that was "detrimental to national security." Following a trial that spanned four months, on June 23, 2014, all three individuals were convicted and received sentences ranging from 7 to 10 years in prison. This outcome provoked strong condemnation from various foreign governments and human rights organizations. The day after the verdicts, Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi announced that he would not intervene in the judicial decisions. In February,

Answer:

options.A: On December 29, 2013, three journalists working for the Qatari-based international news channel Al Jazeera English, Australian Peter Greste, Canadian Mohamed Fahmy and Egyptian Baher Mohamed were taken into custody by Egyptian security forces at the Marriott Hotel in Cairo following a raid at their room, which was used for the news channel's remote studio. The Egyptian Interior Ministry confirmed the arrest and said the journalists were accused of reporting false news which was "damaging to national security".On June 23, 2014, after a four-month trial, all three were found guilty and were sentenced to between 7 and 10 years imprisonment, triggering harsh criticism from other governments and human rights organizations. A day later, Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi declared that he would not interfere with judicial rulings.In February
options.B: After Egyptian security forces raided the Marriott Hotel in Cairo on December 29, 2013, three journalists from Al Jazeera English, Australian Peter Greste, Canadian Mohamed Fahmy, and Egyptian Baher Mohamed were arrested and charged with reporting false news that endangered national security. The Egyptian Interior Ministry confirmed the arrest and sentenced all three to seven to ten years in prison after a four-month trial. Other governments and human rights groups expressed outrage and Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi declared that he would not interfere on judicial decisions.
options.C: On the 29th of December, 2013, three journalists, all employed by Al Jazeera English, an international news channel based in Qatar, were apprehended by Egyptian security personnel. These individuals were Peter Greste, an Australian citizen, Mohamed Fahmy, a Canadian national, and Baher Mohamed, an Egyptian. Their apprehension occurred at the Marriott Hotel in Cairo, subsequent to a search of their room, which had been serving as a makeshift studio for the news channel. The Egyptian Interior Ministry subsequently verified the arrests, stating that the journalists faced allegations of disseminating inaccurate information that was "detrimental to national security." Following a trial that spanned four months, on June 23, 2014, all three individuals were convicted and received sentences ranging from 7 to 10 years in prison. This outcome provoked strong condemnation from various foreign governments and human rights organizations.
